questions.question: The joint PMF of two discrete random variables X and Y is given by pXY(x,y) = kxy for x = 1,2,3 and y = 1,2,3. What is the value of the constant k?
The joint PMF of two discrete random variables X and Y is given by pXY(x,y) = (1/36)xy for x = 1,2,3 and y = 1,2,3. What is the marginal PMF of X?
The joint PMF of two discrete random variables X and Y is given by pXY(x,y) = (1/36)xy for x = 1,2,3 and y = 1,2,3. What is the probability P(1 ≤ X ≤ 2, Y ≤ 2)?
A fair coin is tossed three times. Let X be the number of heads in the first two tosses, and let Y be the number of heads in the third toss. What is the joint PMF pXY(1,1)?
Two random variables X and Y have the joint PMF: pXY(1,1)=0.10, pXY(2,2)=0.35, pXY(3,3)=0.05, and pXY(4,4)=0.50. What is the value of the joint CDF FXY(2,2)?
Two random variables X and Y have the joint PMF: pXY(1,1)=0.10, pXY(2,2)=0.35, pXY(3,3)=0.05, and pXY(4,4)=0.50. What is P(1 ≤ X ≤ 2, Y ≤ 2)?
Two random variables X and Y have the joint PMF: pXY(1,1)=0.10, pXY(2,2)=0.35, pXY(3,3)=0.05, and pXY(4,4)=0.50. What is the value of the joint CDF FXY(1,2)?(من عندي)
Two discrete random variables X and Y have the joint CDF values: FXY(0,0)=1/12, FXY(0,1)=1/3, FXY(0,2)=2/3, FXY(1,0)=1/6, FXY(1,1)=7/12, and FXY(1,2)=1. What is P(0 < X < 2, 0 < Y < 2)?
Two discrete random variables X and Y have the joint CDF values: FXY(0,0)=1/12, FXY(0,1)=1/3, FXY(0,2)=2/3, FXY(1,0)=1/6, FXY(1,1)=7/12, and FXY(1,2)=1. What is the marginal CDF FX(1)?
Two discrete random variables X and Y have the joint CDF values: FXY(0,0)=1/12, FXY(0,1)=1/3, FXY(0,2)=2/3, FXY(1,0)=1/6, FXY(1,1)=7/12, and FXY(1,2)=1. What is P(X=1,Y=1)?

Two discrete random variables X and Y have the joint PMF: p(1,1)=1/12, p(1,2)=1/6, p(1,3)=1/12, p(2,1)=1/6, p(2,2)=1/4, p(2,3)=1/12, p(3,1)=1/12, and all other values are 0. What is the marginal PMF pX(1)?
Two discrete random variables X and Y have the joint PMF: p(1,1)=1/12, p(1,2)=1/6, p(1,3)=1/12, p(2,1)=1/6, p(2,2)=1/4, p(2,3)=1/12, p(3,1)=1/12, and all other values are 0. What is P(X < 2.5)?

Two discrete random variables X and Y have the joint PMF: p(1,1)=1/12, p(1,2)=1/6, p(1,3)=1/12, p(2,1)=1/6, p(2,2)=1/4, p(2,3)=1/12, p(3,1)=1/12, and all other values are 0. What is the probability that Y is odd?
Two discrete random variables X and Y have the joint PMF: p(1,1)=0.2, p(1,2)=0.1, p(2,1)=0.1, p(2,2)=0.2, p(3,1)=0.1, and p(3,2)=0.3. What is the marginal PMF pX(2)?
Two discrete random variables X and Y have the joint PMF: p(1,1)=0.2, p(1,2)=0.1, p(2,1)=0.1, p(2,2)=0.2, p(3,1)=0.1, and p(3,2)=0.3. What is the conditional probability pX|Y(1|2)?
Two discrete random variables X and Y have the joint PMF: p(1,1)=0.2, p(1,2)=0.1, p(2,1)=0.1, p(2,2)=0.2, p(3,1)=0.1, and p(3,2)=0.3. Are X and Y independent?
options: Yes, because pXY(x,y)=pX(x)pY(y) for all x and y
No, because pXY(x,y)≠pX(x)pY(y) for all x and y
No, because X and Y have different ranges
questions.answer: No, because pXY(x,y)≠pX(x)pY(y) for all x and y

questions.question: The joint PDF of two continuous random variables X and Y is fXY(x,y) = kx for 0 < y ≤ x < 1 and 0 otherwise. What is the value of k?
The joint PDF of two continuous random variables X and Y is fXY(x,y) = 3x for 0 < y ≤ x < 1. What is the marginal PDF fX(x)?
The joint CDF of two continuous random variables is FXY(x,y) = 1 − e^(−ax)e^(−by) + e^(−(ax+by)) for x ≥ 0, y ≥ 0. What is the marginal PDF of X?
The joint CDF is FXY(x,y) = 1 − e^(−ax)e^(−by). What is the marginal PDF of Y?
